raw_body:
{"update":"I need help deciding where to document the communications architecture for TrueSight DAO / Agroverse so that members and newcomers can get oriented on where to find what.

## Context

I've just restructured how information flows in the project. Previously the Beer Hall WhatsApp channel was used for broadcast updates, but feedback revealed most members weren't consuming it and it was generating significant production burden for little value. The new architecture splits content by type and audience:

1. **Framework / technical / reflective content** → published on the TrueSight blog at https://truesight.me/blog/index.html. Examples include posts on sweat equity, cooperative profit sharing, warehousing economics, DAO evolution post-LLM, coordination philosophy.

2. **Operational updates (inventory, shipments, milestones, logistics)** → auto-compiled daily at 00:00 UTC on the landing page. May add a chatbot layer later for natural-language context retrieval over the underlying ledger / Google Sheets / change logs.

3. **Beer Hall WhatsApp channel** → default state is empty. Reactivates only when someone (me or any member) wants to surface a new signal for collective consideration. No more routine updates. The bar for posting rises naturally.

4. **Executor WhatsApp channels** (Agroverse supply chain, design/costing/manufacturing) → unchanged. Real-time operational coordination with actual operators (Kirsten, Fatima, Matheus, etc.).

5. **Direct messages to small core** → personal relationship and material updates as needed, not broadcast.

## Relevant repositories and pages

- Whitepaper: https://truesight.me/whitepaper/ (Section "Sweat equity, cooperatives, and the DAO label" already names the structural shape; Section "Chatrooms" describes channel setup)
- Blog: https://truesight.me/blog/index.html
- Landing page with daily compiled updates: [landing page URL]
- ecosystem_change_logs repo: https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/ecosystem_change_logs
- go_to_market repo: https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market
- FAQ: https://truesight.me/faq.html

## What I need help with

Recommend where to publish a short orientation document that explains this architecture to members and newcomers, such that someone encountering the project knows where to find what. Considerations:

- Should it be a new blog post on truesight.me/blog?
- Should it be added to the FAQ?
- Should it be added as a new section to the whitepaper (e.g., under "Chatrooms")?
- Should it live as a pinned message / README in the Beer Hall itself, so anyone visiting the mostly-empty channel sees the explanation?
- Some combination of the above?

Please review the whitepaper's existing "Chatrooms" section and the FAQ structure to recommend the least-duplicative, most-discoverable placement. If you recommend multiple placements, specify what the content should be at each location (e.g., short pinned message vs. longer canonical reference).

Also draft the actual orientation content for whichever location(s) you recommend. Keep it concise, operator-tone, no marketing fluff — in the style of the existing whitepaper and blog posts.

Finally, flag any existing content on the site that will become stale or contradictory with this new architecture and should be updated or deprecated."}
